title: The Bedrock Plumbing

features.name: foundational-pipes
features.desc-on-touch: Original copper tubing, laid before the motel's walls existed. The machines that relied on this water are long gone, but the metal remembers every temperature it carried.

features.name: pressure-regulator-fossil
features.desc-on-touch: A valve corroded into permanence, stuck between open and closed. It remembers balancing competing needs: hot water for guests, cold water for the building's own thirst.

features.name: sediment-layer-archive
features.desc-on-touch: Minerals and detritus settled at the lowest point. The archive recognized it as stratified memory: each layer a year of operation, what flowed through, what stayed behind.

features.name: junction-where-systems-met
features.desc-on-touch: Where the motel's supply line meets the building's original well connection. Two water-stories braiding into one, the junction still holding both pressures.

features.name: thermal-signature-gauge
features.desc-on-touch: A gauge that read temperature continuously. The motel's thermal record lives in this needle's position: at what hour the water ran hot, at what hour it cooled.

features.name: drainage-memory-sump
features.desc-on-touch: The lowest chamber, where every flow eventually rested. The archive found it contained: hair, coins, fragments of soap labels, a single earring, and water that still dreams of its guests.

inscription: the machines kept better records than the guest book; below ground, the motel's true temperature remains constant

file.name: Motel_Mechanical_System_OriginalPlans_1967_WithAnnotations.dwg
file.note: Blueprint of plumbing and thermal systems, marked up with handwritten corrections that contradict printed specifications. Later annotations show the systems slowly modifying their own architecture.

exits.label: back up to the motel's machine memory where the walls still stand
deeper underground where the original well still breathes
east to where the sewage system remembers becoming
